attributes.description: Alerts when `etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes / etcd_server_quota_backend_bytes > 0.9`. Hitting quota puts etcd into alarm mode and rejects writes.
attributes.name: [etcd OTel] Database size approaching quota

investigation_guide.blob:
## etcd database size approaching quota

### What fired
DB size exceeded 90% of the configured backend quota.

### Why it matters
When etcd hits its quota it raises NOSPACE and stops accepting writes until the operator clears the alarm. Kubernetes and other clients will stall.

### Triage
1. Confirm the ratio on the Storage dashboard.
2. Check defragmentation status and compaction schedule.
3. Inspect key counts per prefix (if known) to spot runaway data.

### Remediation
- Run `etcdctl defrag` on each member.
- Issue `etcdctl alarm disarm` once space is reclaimed.
- Raise `--quota-backend-bytes` if workload has grown legitimately.

### Tuning
- 0.9 is a late threshold — consider a 0.7 warning rule as well.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-etcd.otel-default*
// DB size vs quota: when ratio reaches 1, etcd enters alarm mode and rejects writes
| WHERE etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes IS NOT NULL
  AND etcd_server_quota_backend_bytes IS NOT NULL
  AND etcd_server_quota_backend_bytes > 0
| EVAL usage_ratio = etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes * 1.0 / etcd_server_quota_backend_bytes
// Alert when usage exceeds 90%; adjust threshold for earlier warning
| WHERE usage_ratio > 0.9
| STATS usage_ratio = MAX(usage_ratio),
    db_bytes = MAX(etcd_mvcc_db_total_size_in_bytes),
    quota_bytes = MAX(etcd_server_quota_backend_bytes)
    BY resource.attributes.service.instance.id
| SORT usage_ratio DESC
